project.description:
Verify a PIC-SURE environment is healthy by running the pic-sure-python-adapter-hpds live integration tests against it (connect/PSAMA, dictionary search, facets, legacy + v3 queries, export, genomic). A failing build means the environment is NOT fit to baseline, cut over, or promote.
REQUIRES a one-time setup step: a "Secret text" credential with ID picsure-health-check-token holding a long-term PSAMA token for the target environment. Full setup and usage guide: docs/health-check.md in the pic-sure-all-in-one repo.
Stages: 1. HEALTH GATE (always) — pytest tests/integration inside a throwaway uv/python container. Any failure fails the build. 2. METRICS (optional, collect_metrics=true) — scripts/collect_env_metrics.py samples every route N times and archives per-route p50/p95/p99 as build artifacts (metrics-results/).
Targeting: leave target_url empty to auto-target the LOCAL all-in-one (the ServerName from the deployed httpd vhosts; the container trusts the deployed cert chain and resolves the FQDN to this host). Set target_url to check a remote environment with a public cert — pair it with a token valid THERE.
Tests that need environment-specific inputs (concept_path, gene) skip cleanly when the parameter is empty — set them to widen coverage on environments where you know valid values.

hudson.model.StringParameterDefinition.name: git_hash
hudson.model.StringParameterDefinition.description: pic-sure-python-adapter-hpds revision to run the suite from

hudson.model.StringParameterDefinition.name: target_url
hudson.model.StringParameterDefinition.description: Base URL of the environment to check (e.g. https://bdc-predev.example.org). LEAVE EMPTY to auto-target the local all-in-one via the deployed httpd ServerName + cert chain.

hudson.model.StringParameterDefinition.name: concept_path
hudson.model.StringParameterDefinition.description: Concept path that exists on the target dataset (e.g. \1000Genomes\open_access-1000Genomes\SIMULATED AGE\). Empty = query/export tests skip.

hudson.model.StringParameterDefinition.name: search_term
hudson.model.StringParameterDefinition.description: Dictionary search term; "age" exists on most deployments

hudson.model.StringParameterDefinition.name: gene
hudson.model.StringParameterDefinition.description: Gene symbol present in the target's variant data (e.g. CHD8). Empty = genomic tests skip.

hudson.model.StringParameterDefinition.name: resource_uuid
hudson.model.StringParameterDefinition.description: Only needed when the target exposes more than one resource

hudson.model.BooleanParameterDefinition.name: collect_metrics
hudson.model.BooleanParameterDefinition.description: Also collect per-route p50/p95/p99 latency and archive it as build artifacts

hudson.model.StringParameterDefinition.name: metrics_label
hudson.model.StringParameterDefinition.description: Label for the metrics run. Empty = auto-detect wildfly-direct / via-gateway from the deployed httpd rules (local only).

hudson.tasks.Shell.command:
#!/bin/bash set -e
# ============================================================================ # PIC-SURE Health Check # # Runs the python adapter's LIVE integration suite against a PIC-SURE # environment inside a throwaway uv/python container (docker sibling, house # style — the Jenkins container itself has no python toolchain). # # Secrets: PICSURE_TOKEN is injected by the credentials-binding build wrapper # from the "Secret text" credential with ID picsure-health-check-token. # It is passed to the sibling container as an env var and never echoed # (this script has a shebang, so Jenkins does not run it with -x). # # Full documentation: docs/health-check.md in pic-sure-all-in-one. # ============================================================================
CONFIG_DIR=/usr/local/docker-config # The adapter's pinned interpreter is 3.10 (.python-version); keep in sync. IMAGE=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:python3.10-bookworm-slim
if [ -z "$PICSURE_TOKEN" ]; then echo "ERROR: PICSURE_TOKEN is empty." echo "Add a 'Secret text' credential with ID picsure-health-check-token" echo "(Manage Jenkins -> Credentials). See docs/health-check.md." exit 2 fi
# ---- resolve the target environment --------------------------------------- # Local mode (target_url empty): the stack's FQDN comes from the deployed # vhosts. Containers don't inherit the host's /etc/hosts, so the FQDN is # mapped to this docker host (host-gateway -> httpd's published :443), and # python is pointed at the deployed cert chain (httpx trusts only certifi # unless SSL_CERT_FILE says otherwise — mkcert/self-signed certs need this). if [ -n "$target_url" ]; then TARGET_URL="$target_url" LOCAL_TARGET=0 else SERVER_NAME=$(grep -m1 -E '^[[:space:]]*ServerName' "$CONFIG_DIR/httpd/httpd-vhosts.conf" | awk '{print $2}') if [ -z "$SERVER_NAME" ]; then echo "ERROR: could not read ServerName from $CONFIG_DIR/httpd/httpd-vhosts.conf." echo "Pass target_url explicitly instead." exit 2 fi TARGET_URL="https://$SERVER_NAME" LOCAL_TARGET=1 fi echo "Target: $TARGET_URL (local=$LOCAL_TARGET)"
# ---- shared container arguments ------------------------------------------- # Workspace is mounted via its HOST path (docker-out-of-docker convention); # uv_cache persists downloads across builds like maven_m2_cache does. DOCKER_ARGS=( --rm -v "$DOCKER_CONFIG_DIR/jenkins_home/workspace/$JOB_NAME/:/workspace" -w /workspace -v uv_cache:/opt/uv-cache -e UV_CACHE_DIR=/opt/uv-cache -e UV_PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT=/opt/venv -e PICSURE_INTEGRATION=1 -e PICSURE_TEST_PLATFORM="$TARGET_URL" -e PICSURE_TEST_TOKEN="$PICSURE_TOKEN" -e PICSURE_TEST_CONCEPT_PATH="$concept_path" -e PICSURE_TEST_SEARCH_TERM="$search_term" -e PICSURE_TEST_GENE="$gene" -e PICSURE_TEST_RESOURCE_UUID="$resource_uuid" ) if [ "$LOCAL_TARGET" = "1" ]; then FQDN="${TARGET_URL#https://}" DOCKER_ARGS+=( --add-host "$FQDN:host-gateway" ) DOCKER_ARGS+=( -v "$DOCKER_CONFIG_DIR/httpd/cert/server.chain:/opt/ca-chain.pem:ro" -e SSL_CERT_FILE=/opt/ca-chain.pem ) fi
# ---- stage 1: health gate (always) ----------------------------------------- echo "== Stage 1/2: health gate (adapter live integration tests) ==" if ! docker run "${DOCKER_ARGS[@]}" --entrypoint /bin/sh "$IMAGE" \ -c 'uv sync --frozen && uv run pytest tests/integration -q'; then echo "" echo "HEALTH GATE FAILED: $TARGET_URL failed live integration tests." echo "Do NOT baseline, cut over, or promote until this passes." echo "Common causes: expired token (regenerate + update the Jenkins" echo "credential), a stopped container, or a broken route — see the" echo "pytest output above for which route family failed." exit 1 fi echo "Health gate PASSED for $TARGET_URL"
# ---- stage 2: per-route latency metrics (optional) -------------------------- if [ "$collect_metrics" = "true" ]; then LABEL="$metrics_label" if [ -z "$LABEL" ]; then # Which way is the local httpd routing /picsure right now? if grep -F 'http://gateway:8080/$1' "$CONFIG_DIR/httpd/httpd-vhosts.conf" 2>/dev/null | grep -qv '^[[:space:]]*#'; then LABEL=via-gateway else LABEL=wildfly-direct fi fi echo "== Stage 2/2: per-route latency metrics (label=adapter-$LABEL) ==" docker run "${DOCKER_ARGS[@]}" --entrypoint /bin/sh "$IMAGE" \ -c "uv sync --frozen && uv run python scripts/collect_env_metrics.py --label 'adapter-$LABEL' --out /workspace/metrics-results" echo "Metrics summary archived as build artifacts (metrics-results/)." else echo "collect_metrics=false — skipping metrics stage." fi
